title: The Evening Anchor: Bath, Hot Drink, Slow Exhale
subtitle: A three-part ritual for putting the body down for the evening
excerpt: A simple three-part evening ritual for transitioning the body from the day into rest. Bath or warm water, a hot drink, and a slow exhale: three physical anchors that signal the evening is for you.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Draws on established sleep-hygiene and somatic settling practice.

practice.whenToUse: Use as a closing ritual at the end of the day, especially on evenings when the body is still running and rest feels out of reach.

content.text: A three-part evening ritual for putting the body down. It takes ten minutes and requires nothing special. The three anchors work because each one is a clear physical signal: the day is ending, the evening is yours.
Choose an evening this week to run the full sequence. Have a drink you like ready, something warm. The bath can be a short one or a footbath if a full bath is not practical.
I am ready to release the day. I release it now. I release it now. I release it now.
Say this while you are in the bath or footbath, or while you run the water. Say it three times.
I am ready to allow the evening. I allow it now. I allow it now. I allow it now.
Say this while you make the hot drink or wrap a towel around yourself.
Sit with the hot drink. Take three slow exhales, longer on the out-breath than the in-breath. No counting required. Just longer out. Do this without your phone in your hand.
Say this once at the end. Write it down if you want to carry it into tomorrow.
Original to homemade.education. It draws on sleep research and the use of physical cues to settle the body.
